title: Cadeau Staying at Michigan After Jim Harbaugh's Assistant Coach Exit
body:
In a significant development for the Michigan Wolverines men's basketball program, guard Elliot Cadeau has announced his intention to remain with the team despite the recent departure of head coach Dusty May. This news comes as a welcome relief for Michigan fans, who had been bracing themselves for potential roster upheaval in the wake of May's exit. Cadeau, who started all 40 games for the Wolverines last season, will provide a much-needed sense of continuity and stability as the program navigates this period of transition.

The decision by Cadeau to stay put is a testament to the strong bonds he has formed with his teammates and the Michigan coaching staff, despite the change at the helm. His agent, Drew Gross, confirmed that Cadeau is committed to seeing out his collegiate career with the Wolverines, and will not be exploring transfer options or entering the professional draft at this time. This commitment is a significant boost for Michigan, which will be looking to rebuild and retool under new leadership. With Cadeau on board, the Wolverines will have a seasoned and experienced player to anchor their backcourt, and provide guidance and mentorship to younger teammates.

The departure of Dusty May had sent shockwaves through the Michigan basketball community, with many fans and observers wondering how the team would respond to the loss of their head coach. May's tenure had been marked by significant on-court success, and his exit had raised questions about the program's ability to attract and retain top talent. However, Cadeau's decision to stay suggests that the Wolverines' recruiting class and existing roster are still strongly invested in the program's vision and values. This bodes well for the team's future prospects, as they look to regroup and refocus under new leadership.

Cadeau's own development and growth as a player will be an interesting subplot to watch in the coming season. As a key contributor to the Wolverines' lineup, he will be expected to take on additional leadership responsibilities and help drive the team's offense. With his experience and skillset, Cadeau is well-equipped to thrive in this role, and could potentially emerge as one of the top players in the conference. His decision to stay at Michigan also speaks to the strong relationships he has built with his teammates, and the sense of camaraderie and esprit de corps that exists within the program.

As the Wolverines look to move forward and put the uncertainty of the past few weeks behind them, Cadeau's commitment provides a sense of stability and normalcy. The program will still face significant challenges in the coming season, including the need to adjust to a new coaching staff and system. However, with Cadeau on board, Michigan will have a foundation to build upon, and a core of experienced players who can help drive the team's success. As the college basketball landscape continues to evolve and shift, the Wolverines will be looking to establish themselves as a consistent and competitive force in the conference.

Looking ahead to the future, the Wolverines will be eager to put the distractions of the past few weeks behind them and focus on the task at hand: rebuilding and retooling for the upcoming season. With Cadeau's decision to stay, Michigan has taken a significant step in the right direction, and will be looking to build upon this momentum in the weeks and months to come. As the program navigates this period of transition, one thing is clear: the Wolverines will be a team to watch in the coming season, with Cadeau leading the charge and driving their push for success.
